content.problemStatement:
--

**Write a class "Pizza" with overloaded constructors to prepare a pizza with different combinations of attributes like size, crust, toppings, and price.**

- Define the following instance variables (attributes) for the Pizza class with appropriate data types:
   - size (e.g. Small, Medium, Large)
   - crust type (e.g. Thin, Pan, Stuffed)
   - toppings (an array of Strings, e.g. Pepperoni, Mushrooms, Onions, etc.)
   - price (a double; optional, defaults to 0 if not specified)
- Create overloaded constructors for the Pizza class that account for different combinations of properties:
   - A constructor that takes the size and the crust type as arguments.
   - A constructor that takes the size, crust type, and toppings (an array of Strings) as arguments.
- Create a method inside the Pizza class called "displayPizzaInfo" that prints the property values of the pizza object in a user-friendly format.
- Create a main method demonstrating the usage of the Pizza class with different constructor calls and displaying their information.

--
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
